content.full_text:
BEN ADAMS
2019 NORTH BEVERLY DRIVE
BEVERLY HILLS, CA 90210

DECEMBER 18, 1992

JEANETTE TURVILL
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J STREET, SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814

DEAR JEANETTE,

I AM WRITING YOU THIS LETTER AS I AM A MEMBER, AREA BOARD X ON
DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES (DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES BOARD, AREA X). I
AM CONCERNED ABOUT POSSIBLE CONFLICTS OF INTEREST IN MY POSITION AND I
WOULD LIKE AN OPINION ON THESE. BEING THAT I SERVE ON A VOLUNTARY
BASIS, THE COST OF COUNSEL WOULD BE PROHIBITIVE AND WOULD MAKE IT EVEN
MORE DIFFICULT TO RECRUIT NEW BOARD MEMBERS.

MY QUESTION IS: CAN I PERFORM INDIVIDUAL ADVOCACY FOR A PARENT AND
THEIR CHILD WITH A DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITY AND RECEIVE PAYMENT FOR MY
SERVICES AS LONG AS I DO NOT ACT ON THEIR BEHALF WITH THE DEVELOPMENTAL
DISABILITIES BOARD, THAT I INFORM THEM I AM A BOARD MEMBER, AND I STAY
OUT OF THE DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES BOARD DIALOGUE AND/OR DECISIONS
CONCERNING THAT CLIENT?

I WOULD SINCERELY APPRECIATE AN ANSWER TO THIS QUESTION.

THANK YOU,

BEN ADAMS

AUG 17 1992

VED

BEN ADAMS
2019 NORTH BEVERLY DRIVE
BEVERLY HILLS, CA 90210

AUGUST 12, 1992

JEANETTE TURVILL
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J STREET, SUITE 800
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814

DEAR JEANETTE,

I AM WRITING YOU THIS LETTER AS I AM A MEMBER, AREA BOARD X ON
DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES BOARD, AREA X). I AM CONCERNED ABOUT POSSIBLE CONFLICTS OF INTEREST IN MY POSITION AND I
WOULD LIKE AN OPINION ON THESE. BEING THAT I SERVE ON A VOLUNTARY
BASIS, THE COST OF COUNSEL WOULD BE PROHIBITIVE AND WOULD MAKE IT EVEN
MORE DIFFICULT TO RECRUIT NEW BOARD MEMBERS. THE ISSUES:

1) I OWN 6 APARTMENT UNITS. CAN I RENT TO A CONSUMER WITH
DEVELOPMENTAL DISABILITIES IF THAT PERSON IS RECEIVING SUPPORT,
FINANCIAL OR OTHERWISE, FROM A REGIONAL CENTER? I NOTE THAT WITH 6
UNITS EACH TENANT REPRESENTS OVER 10% OF MY CLIENTELE.

2) CAN I ACCEPT A DEPOSIT ON A RENTAL FROM A REGIONAL CENTER? CAN
THE REGIONAL CENTER GUARANTEE THAT THE RENT WILL BE PAID?

3) CAN I BECOME A MEMBER OF THE LOCAL SCHOOL DISTRICT'S SPECIAL
EDUCATION COMMISSION? THE SPECIAL EDUCATION COMMISSION OF THE LOS
ANGELES UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT WAS ESTABLISHED BY THE BOARD OF
EDUCATION TO ADVISE, ASSIST, AND MAKE RECOMMENDATIONS REGARDING
PROGRAMS, SERVICES AND ISSUES RELATED TO PEOPLE WITH DISABILITIES. IT
IS PURELY VOLUNTARY IN NATURE WITH NO PAY. THE COMMISSION, WHICH IS
STAFFED BY A BOARD-APPOINTED DIRECTOR WHO IS A CERTIFICATED EMPLOYEE,
ACTS IN A LIAISON CAPACITY BY ADVISING AND REFERRING PARENTS AND THE
COMMUNITY FOR HELP IN SOLVING PROBLEMS RELATED TO THE NEEDS OF PEOPLE
WITH DISABILITIES. THE COMMISSION HAS RECRUITED ME FOR MEMBERSHIP.

4) CAN MY SPOUSE SERVE ON THE BOARD OF A REGIONAL CENTER OR OTHER
STATE OR FEDERALLY FUNDED AGENCY THAT ADVOCATES FOR PERSONS WITH
DEVELOPEMENTAL DISABILITIES IN LOS ANGELES COUNTY?

I WOULD SINCERELY APPRECIATE AN ANSWER TO THESE QUESTIONS.

THANK YOU,

BEN ADAMS

[This image appears to be a very faded or low-contrast scan of a document. The text is extremely difficult to read with confidence. Most of the content is illegible due to the poor image quality, with only scattered fragments of text barely visible throughout the page. Without being able to reliably make out the specific words and sentences, I cannot provide an accurate transcription while following the instruction to copy text verbatim.]
